mechanism_narrative: Low and stable federal funds rates indicate an accommodative monetary policy, which reduces the cost of capital for businesses and supports equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: any regime with stable low rates
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in more than 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS is below 3.5% and stable (standard deviation < 0.1% over 3 months).

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic slowdown, which negatively impacts corporate earnings and drives down S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 40% of triggered instances despite the negative signal from UNRATE.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales combined with robust housing starts indicate healthy consumer demand and investment, suggesting a strong economic expansion that boosts corporate revenues and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND HOUST > 1800 thousand units (3-month average)
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in more than 50% of periods where both RSAFS and HOUST signals are positive and triggered.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a recovery phase indicates increasing manufacturing activity and capacity utilization, which translates into higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: During post-recession recovery periods, if INDPRO growth is positive but SPX 60d returns are consistently negative (average return < 0) for any 12-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes purchasing power and may trigger aggressive monetary tightening, both of which are detrimental to corporate earnings and lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative inflation signal.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job growth, as indicated by PAYEMS, reflects a healthy and expanding economy, supporting consumer spending and corporate profitability, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in more than 50% of periods where PAYEMS shows consistent positive month-over-month growth (average > 100k over 3 months).

mechanism_narrative: Stagnant or declining GDP growth combined with rising federal funds rates indicates a stagflationary or recessionary environment, where economic activity slows while borrowing costs increase, severely impacting corporate valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth < 1.0% AND FEDFUNDS rises > 0.5 percentage points over 6 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 40% of periods where both GDPC1 and FEDFUNDS signals are negative and triggered.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts during a period of rising interest rates signals a significant slowdown in the housing market and broader economic demand, which typically precedes or accompanies a recession, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 10% over 3 months (3-month average vs. prior 3-month average)
failure_condition: If HOUST declines significantly during a rising rate environment (FEDFUNDS > 3.0% and rising) but SPX 20d returns remain positive for more than 60% of such triggered periods.
